1.1: In the eighth month of the second year of Darius, the word of the LORD came to the prophet Zechariah son of Berechiah son of Iddo:
1.2: The LORD was very angry with your ancestors.
1.3: Therefore say to them, This is what the LORD of hosts says: Return to me, declares the LORD of hosts, and I will return to you, declares the LORD of hosts.
1.4: Do not be like your ancestors, to whom the earlier prophets called: This is what the LORD of hosts says — Turn from your evil ways and your evil deeds. But they did not listen or pay attention to me, declares the LORD.
1.5: Where are your ancestors now? And the prophets — do they live forever?
1.6: But did not my words and my decrees, which I commanded my servants the prophets, overtake your ancestors? So they repented and said, The LORD of hosts has dealt with us according to our ways and deeds, just as he intended.
1.7: On the twenty-fourth day of the eleventh month — the month of Shebat — in the second year of Darius, the word of the LORD came to the prophet Zechariah son of Berechiah son of Iddo.
1.8: During the night I had a vision — a man riding a red horse, standing among the myrtle trees in the ravine, with red, sorrel, and white horses behind him.
1.9: I asked, What are these, my lord? The angel who was speaking with me said, I will show you what they are.
1.10: Then the man standing among the myrtle trees spoke up and said, These are those whom the LORD has sent to patrol throughout the earth.
1.11: They reported to the angel of the LORD who was standing among the myrtle trees, We have patrolled the earth and found the whole earth at rest and peaceful.
1.12: At this the angel of the LORD said, LORD of hosts, how long will you withhold mercy from Jerusalem and the cities of Judah? You have been angry with them for seventy years now.
1.13: The LORD answered the angel who was speaking with me with gracious and comforting words.
1.14: Then the angel who was speaking with me told me, Proclaim this: This is what the LORD of hosts says — I am burning with jealousy for Jerusalem and for Zion.
1.15: But I am very angry with the nations that are complacent; for I was only a little angry, yet they intensified the disaster.
1.16: Therefore, this is what the LORD says: I have returned to Jerusalem with compassion. My house will be rebuilt there, declares the LORD of hosts, and a measuring line will be stretched over Jerusalem.
1.17: Proclaim further: This is what the LORD of hosts says — My cities will again overflow with abundance, and the LORD will again comfort Zion and again choose Jerusalem.
1.18: Then I looked up and saw four horns.
1.19: I asked the angel who was speaking with me, What are these? He answered, These are the horns that scattered Judah, Israel, and Jerusalem.
1.20: Then the LORD showed me four craftsmen.
1.21: I asked, What have these come to do? He said, These are the horns that scattered Judah so that no one could raise his head. Now these craftsmen have come to terrify them and to throw down the horns of the nations that raised their horn against the land of Judah to scatter its people.

2.1: Then I looked up and saw a man with a measuring line in his hand.
2.2: I asked, Where are you going? He answered, To measure Jerusalem — to find out its width and its length.
2.3: Then the angel who had been speaking with me went forward, and another angel came forward to meet him.
2.4: The second angel said, Run and tell that young man: Jerusalem will be filled with people and animals, spread out like open villages without walls.
2.5: For I will be a wall of fire around her, declares the LORD, and I will be the glory within her.
2.6: Come, come! Flee from the land of the north, declares the LORD, for I have scattered you to the four winds of heaven, declares the LORD.
2.7: Come, escape from Babylon, you who live there, daughter of Zion!
2.8: For this is what the LORD of hosts says: After his glory he sent me against the nations that plundered you — for whoever touches you touches the apple of his eye.
2.9: See, I am going to raise my hand against them, and they will become plunder for their own former servants. Then you will know that the LORD of hosts has sent me.
2.10: Shout and rejoice, daughter of Zion! For I am coming, and I will dwell in your midst, declares the LORD.
2.11: Many nations will join themselves to the LORD on that day and will become my people. I will dwell in your midst, and you will know that the LORD of hosts has sent me to you.
2.12: The LORD will take Judah as his portion in the holy land, and will once again choose Jerusalem.
2.13: Let all people be silent before the LORD, for he has stirred from his holy dwelling.

3.1: Then he showed me Joshua the high priest standing before the angel of the LORD, with Satan standing at his right hand to accuse him.
3.2: The LORD said to Satan, The LORD rebuke you, Satan! The LORD who has chosen Jerusalem rebuke you! Is this not a burning stick snatched from the fire?
3.3: Joshua was dressed in filthy garments as he stood before the angel.
3.4: The angel said to those standing before him, Take off his filthy garments. Then he said to Joshua, See, I have taken your guilt away from you, and I will clothe you with fine robes.
3.5: Then I said, Let them put a clean turban on his head. So they put a clean turban on his head and clothed him in the robes, while the angel of the LORD stood nearby.
3.6: Then the angel of the LORD gave Joshua this solemn charge:
3.7: This is what the LORD of hosts says: If you walk in my ways and faithfully keep my requirements, then you will govern my house and have charge of my courts, and I will give you free access among those who stand here.
3.8: Listen, Joshua the high priest — you and your associates who sit before you, for they are men who are a sign. I am going to bring my servant, the Branch.
3.9: See, the stone I have set in front of Joshua — on that one stone are seven eyes. I am going to engrave an inscription on it, declares the LORD of hosts, and I will remove the iniquity of this land in a single day.
3.10: On that day, declares the LORD of hosts, each of you will invite his neighbor under his vine and fig tree.

4.1: Then the angel who had been speaking with me returned and woke me, like someone roused from sleep.
4.2: He asked me, What do you see? I answered, I see a solid gold lampstand with a bowl at the top, seven lamps on it, and seven channels feeding the seven lamps at the top.
4.3: There are also two olive trees beside it — one on the right of the bowl and one on the left.
4.4: I asked the angel who was speaking with me, What are these, my lord?
4.5: The angel who was speaking with me answered, Do you not know what these are? I said, No, my lord.
4.6: He replied, This is the word of the LORD to Zerubbabel: Not by might, nor by power, but by my Spirit, says the LORD of hosts.
4.7: What are you, mighty mountain? Before Zerubbabel you will become level ground! Then he will bring out the capstone with shouts of Grace, grace to it!
4.8: The word of the LORD came to me again:
4.9: The hands of Zerubbabel laid the foundation of this temple; his hands will also complete it. Then you will know that the LORD of hosts has sent me to you.
4.10: Who despises the day of small beginnings? They will rejoice when they see the plumb line in the hand of Zerubbabel. These seven are the eyes of the LORD, ranging over the whole earth.
4.11: Then I asked him, What are these two olive trees on the right and left of the lampstand?
4.12: I asked him again, What are these two olive branches that pour out golden oil through the two golden pipes?
4.13: He said to me, Do you not know what these are? I answered, No, my lord.
4.14: He said, These are the two anointed ones who stand in the presence of the Lord of the whole earth.

5.1: I looked up again and saw a scroll flying through the air.
5.2: He asked, What do you see? I replied, A flying scroll — thirty feet long and fifteen feet wide.
5.3: He told me, This is the curse that goes out over the whole land. According to one side of it, every thief will be removed; according to the other side, everyone who swears falsely will be removed.
5.4: I will send it out, declares the LORD of hosts, and it will enter the house of the thief and the house of whoever swears falsely in my name. It will remain in that house and destroy it, timber and stones alike.
5.5: Then the angel who was speaking with me came forward and said, Look up and see what this is that is appearing.
5.6: I asked, What is it? He said, This is a measuring basket going out. And he added, This represents the iniquity of the people throughout the land.
5.7: Then the heavy lead cover was raised, and there was a woman sitting inside the basket.
5.8: He said, This is Wickedness. Then he pushed her back down into the basket and pressed the heavy lead lid over the opening.
5.9: I looked up and saw two women coming with the wind in their wings — they had wings like a stork's wings — and they lifted the basket up between earth and heaven.
5.10: I asked the angel who was speaking with me, Where are they taking the basket?
5.11: He replied, To the land of Shinar, to build a temple for it. When the temple is ready, the basket will be placed there on its pedestal.

6.1: Again I looked up and saw four chariots coming out from between two mountains — mountains of bronze.
6.2: The first chariot had red horses, the second had black horses,
6.3: the third had white horses, and the fourth had dappled horses — powerful animals.
6.4: I asked the angel who was speaking with me, What are these, my lord?
6.5: The angel answered, These are the four winds of heaven, going out after presenting themselves before the Lord of all the earth.
6.6: The chariot with black horses is heading north, the white horses follow them, and the dappled ones head south.
6.7: When the powerful horses came out, they were straining to go and patrol the earth. He said, Go, patrol the earth. And they went and patrolled it.
6.8: Then he called out to me, See, those who have gone to the north country have given my Spirit rest there.
6.9: Then the word of the LORD came to me:
6.10: Take silver and gold from the exiles — Heldai, Tobijah, and Jedaiah — who have come from Babylon, and go that same day to the house of Josiah son of Zephaniah.
6.11: Take the silver and gold, fashion crowns, and place them on the head of Joshua son of Jehozadak the high priest.
6.12: Then say to him, This is what the LORD of hosts says: Here is the man whose name is the Branch. He will branch out from his own place and build the temple of the LORD.
6.13: He will build the temple of the LORD, and he will be clothed with majesty. He will sit and rule on his throne, and there will also be a priest on his throne. Peaceful counsel will exist between the two of them.
6.14: The crowns will be kept in the temple of the LORD as a memorial to Helem, Tobijah, Jedaiah, and Hen son of Zephaniah.
6.15: Those who are far away will come and help build the temple of the LORD, and you will know that the LORD of hosts has sent me to you. This will come to pass if you diligently obey the LORD your God.

13.1: On that day a fountain will be opened for the house of David and the inhabitants of Jerusalem to cleanse them from sin and impurity.
13.2: On that day, declares the LORD of Hosts, I will remove the names of the idols from the land, so that they will be remembered no more. I will also remove from the land both the prophets and the spirit of uncleanness.
13.3: And if anyone still dares to prophesy, his own father and mother who bore him will say to him, 'You must die, for you have spoken lies in the name of the LORD.' And when he prophesies, his own father and mother will run him through.
13.4: On that day every prophet will be ashamed of his prophetic vision, and they will no longer put on a hairy cloak in order to deceive.
13.5: He will say, 'I am not a prophet. I am a farmer — someone has owned me as a servant since my youth.'
13.6: When someone asks him, 'What are these wounds on your hands?' he will answer, 'Wounds I received in the house of my friends.'
13.7: Awake, O sword, against my shepherd, against the man who is my associate, declares the LORD of Hosts. Strike the shepherd so the sheep will be scattered. I will turn my hand against the little ones.
13.8: Throughout the whole land, declares the LORD, two thirds shall be cut off and die, but one third will be left.
13.9: I will put that third through the fire and refine them as silver is refined, and test them as gold is tested. They will call on my name, and I will answer them. I will say, 'They are my people,' and they will say, 'The LORD is my God.'

14.1: A day is coming for the LORD when the plunder taken from you will be divided up right in your midst.
14.2: I will gather all the nations against Jerusalem to fight. The city will be taken, the houses plundered, the women assaulted. Half the city will go into captivity, but the rest of the people will not be cut off from the city.
14.3: Then the LORD will march out and fight those nations, as he fights in the day of battle.
14.4: On that day his feet will stand on the Mount of Olives, which faces Jerusalem to the east. The Mount of Olives will be split in two from east to west, forming a great valley, with half the mountain moving north and half moving south.
14.5: You will flee through the valley of my mountains, for the valley will extend to Azal. You will flee as you fled from the earthquake in the days of King Uzziah of Judah. Then the LORD my God will come, and all the holy ones with him.
14.6: On that day there will be neither bright light nor heavy darkness.
14.7: It will be one unique day — known only to the LORD — with no distinction between day and night. But at evening time there will be light.
14.8: On that day, living waters will flow out from Jerusalem — half toward the eastern sea and half toward the western sea. This will continue in summer and in winter alike.
14.9: The LORD will be king over all the earth. On that day the LORD will be one and his name one.
14.10: The whole land will be changed into a plain from Geba to Rimmon south of Jerusalem. But Jerusalem will be elevated and will remain in its place, from the Gate of Benjamin to the site of the First Gate, to the Corner Gate, and from the Tower of Hananel to the king's winepresses.
14.11: Jerusalem will be inhabited; there will be no more ban of destruction, and Jerusalem will live in security.
14.12: This will be the plague with which the LORD strikes all the peoples who fought against Jerusalem: their flesh will rot while they are still standing, their eyes will rot in their sockets, and their tongues will rot in their mouths.
14.13: On that day a great panic from the LORD will come upon them: each man will seize the hand of his neighbor, and one will turn against the other.
14.14: Judah also will fight in Jerusalem. The wealth of all the surrounding nations will be gathered — gold, silver, and clothing in great abundance.
14.15: The same plague will strike the horses, mules, camels, donkeys, and all the animals in those camps.
14.16: Then all who survive from the nations that came against Jerusalem will go up year after year to worship the King, the LORD of Hosts, and to celebrate the Feast of Tabernacles.
14.17: If any of the families of the earth do not go up to Jerusalem to worship the King, the LORD of Hosts, no rain will fall on them.
14.18: If the family of Egypt does not go up and come, no rain will fall on them; instead, the LORD will bring the plague he strikes on the nations that do not go up to celebrate the Feast of Tabernacles.
14.19: This will be the punishment for Egypt and for every nation that does not come up to celebrate the Feast of Tabernacles.
14.20: On that day 'Holy to the LORD' will be inscribed on the bells of the horses, and the cooking pots in the LORD's house will be as sacred as the bowls before the altar.
14.21: On that day every pot in Jerusalem and in Judah will be holy to the LORD of Hosts. All who sacrifice will be able to take one and boil their offerings in it. And there will no longer be a trader in the house of the LORD of Hosts.

7.1: In the fourth year of King Darius, on the fourth day of the ninth month — Chislev — the word of the LORD came to Zechariah.
7.2: The people of Bethel had sent Sharezer and Regemmelech, together with their men, to seek the LORD's favor,
7.3: to speak to the priests of the house of the LORD of Hosts and to the prophets with this question: 'Should I mourn and fast in the fifth month as I have done for so many years?'
7.4: Then the word of the LORD of Hosts came to me:
7.5: 'Tell all the people of the land and the priests: When you fasted and mourned in the fifth and the seventh months for these seventy years, was it truly for me that you were fasting?'
7.6: When you eat and when you drink, is it not for yourselves that you eat and drink?
7.7: Are these not the words the LORD announced through the earlier prophets when Jerusalem was inhabited and prosperous, along with the surrounding towns, the Negev, and the Shephelah?
7.8: The word of the LORD came to Zechariah:
7.9: This is what the LORD of Hosts says: Administer true justice; show steadfast love and compassion to one another.
7.10: Do not oppress the widow or the fatherless, the immigrant or the poor. Do not plot evil in your hearts against your neighbor.
7.11: But they refused to listen; they turned a stubborn shoulder and stopped their ears against hearing.
7.12: They made their hearts as hard as flint, refusing to hear the law and the words that the LORD of Hosts sent by his Spirit through the former prophets. So the LORD of Hosts poured out his fierce anger.
7.13: Just as they did not listen when I called, so I did not listen when they called, says the LORD of Hosts,
7.14: so I scattered them with a whirlwind among all the nations they had not known. The land they left behind was desolated — no one passed through, no one came back — for they had turned a delightful land into wasteland.

8.1: The word of the LORD of Hosts came:
8.2: This is what the LORD of Hosts says: I am burning with jealousy for Zion — great jealousy — and I am stirred with great fury on her behalf.
8.3: This is what the LORD says: I have returned to Zion and will live in Jerusalem. Jerusalem will be called the faithful city, and the mountain of the LORD of Hosts will be called the holy mountain.
8.4: This is what the LORD of Hosts says: Old men and old women will once again sit in the streets of Jerusalem, each with a staff in hand because of their age.
8.5: The streets of the city will be full of boys and girls playing in the streets.
8.6: This is what the LORD of Hosts says: Even if this seems impossible to the remnant of this people in those days, should it therefore seem impossible to me? declares the LORD of Hosts.
8.7: This is what the LORD of Hosts says: I will rescue my people from the lands of the east and the lands of the west.
8.8: I will bring them home to live in Jerusalem. They will be my people, and I will be their God, in faithfulness and righteousness.
8.9: This is what the LORD of Hosts says: Be strong, all of you who are hearing these words today from the prophets who were present when the foundation of the house of the LORD of Hosts was laid, so that the temple might be built.
8.10: Before those days, there was no wages for people or animals, no security for anyone who came or went — because I had set everyone against their neighbors.
8.11: But from now on I will not deal with the remnant of this people as I did in the past, declares the LORD of Hosts.
8.12: For there will be a sowing of peace. The vine will yield its fruit, the ground will produce its crops, and the sky will give its dew. I will cause the remnant of this people to inherit all these things.
8.13: Just as you, house of Judah and house of Israel, were a curse among the nations, so I will save you, and you will be a blessing. Do not be afraid; let your hands be strong.
8.14: For this is what the LORD of Hosts says: Just as I determined to bring disaster upon you when your ancestors provoked me to anger, and I did not relent, says the LORD of Hosts,
8.15: so in these days I have determined again to do good to Jerusalem and the house of Judah. Do not be afraid.
8.16: These are the things you must do: Speak the truth to one another. Render honest judgments that lead to peace in your courts.
8.17: Do not plot evil against your neighbor in your heart, and do not love perjury, for all these things I hate, declares the LORD.
8.18: The word of the LORD of Hosts came to me:
8.19: This is what the LORD of Hosts says: The fasts of the fourth, fifth, seventh, and tenth months will become occasions of joy, gladness, and happy festivals for the house of Judah. So love truth and peace.
8.20: This is what the LORD of Hosts says: Peoples will yet come — inhabitants of many cities —
8.21: The inhabitants of one city will go to another, saying, 'Let us go immediately to seek the LORD's favor and to seek the LORD of Hosts. I'm going too.'
8.22: Many peoples and powerful nations will come to seek the LORD of Hosts in Jerusalem and to seek the LORD's favor.
8.23: This is what the LORD of Hosts says: In those days ten men from every nation and language will take hold of the robe of one Jew, saying, 'Let us go with you, for we have heard that God is with you.'

9.1: An oracle — the word of the LORD against the land of Hadrach; Damascus is where it comes to rest. For the eyes of all humanity, and of all Israel's tribes, are fixed on the LORD,
9.2: and also Hamath, which borders Damascus, Tyre and Sidon, though they are very clever.
9.3: Tyre has built herself a fortress and amassed silver like dust and gold like the mud of the streets.
9.4: But look — the Lord will dispossess her and hurl her wealth into the sea, and she will be consumed by fire.
9.5: Ashkelon will see it and be terrified; Gaza too, writhing in anguish; and Ekron, for its hope has withered. Gaza will lose its king, and Ashkelon will be deserted.
9.6: a mixed people will settle in Ashdod, and I will cut off Philistia's pride.
9.7: I will strip the blood from its mouth and the forbidden offerings from between its teeth. Even they will belong to our God; they will become like a clan within Judah, and Ekron will be like the Jebusites.
9.8: I will take up position around my house as a garrison, so that no army marches through it again. No oppressor will overrun them again, for I am watching with my own eyes.
9.9: Rejoice greatly, Daughter Zion! Shout aloud, Daughter Jerusalem! See — your king is coming to you, righteous and victorious, humble and riding on a donkey, on a colt, the foal of a donkey.
9.10: He will cut off the chariot from Ephraim and the war horse from Jerusalem; the battle bow will be cut off. He will proclaim peace to the nations; his dominion will extend from sea to sea, from the River to the ends of the earth.
9.11: As for you — because of the blood of my covenant with you — I will free your prisoners from the waterless pit.
9.12: Return to the stronghold, you prisoners who still have hope; today I declare that I will restore double to you.
9.13: For I have bent Judah like a bow; I have made Ephraim its arrow. I will rouse your sons, Zion, against the sons of Greece, and wield you like a warrior's sword.
9.14: The LORD will appear over them; his arrow will flash like lightning. The Lord GOD will sound the trumpet and advance in the windstorms of the south.
9.15: The LORD of Hosts will shield them. They will devour and trample the sling stones; they will drink and shout as with wine, full as a sacrificial basin, drenched like the corners of the altar.
9.16: On that day the LORD their God will save them as his people's flock. They will gleam like gems in a crown over his land.
9.17: How great is his goodness, how great is his beauty! Grain will strengthen the young men, and new wine will cheer the young women.

10.1: Ask rain from the LORD in the season of the spring rain. The LORD is the one who makes the storm clouds; he will give them showers of rain, growth to everyone in the field.
10.2: For the household idols speak worthlessness, and the diviners see false visions; they tell lying dreams and give empty comfort. Therefore the people wander like sheep; they suffer because there is no shepherd.
10.3: My anger burns against the shepherds, and I will punish the leaders; for the LORD of Hosts tends his flock, the house of Judah, and will make them like his majestic steed in battle.
10.4: From Judah will come the cornerstone, from him the tent peg, from him the battle bow, from him every ruler — all of them together.
10.5: Together they will be like mighty warriors in battle, trampling their enemies in the street mud. They will fight because the LORD is with them, and they will put the horsemen to shame.
10.6: I will strengthen the house of Judah and save the house of Joseph. I will bring them back, for I have compassion on them. They will be as though I had never rejected them, for I am the LORD their God and I will answer them.
10.7: Ephraim will be like a mighty warrior, and their hearts will rejoice as with wine. Their children will see it and rejoice; their hearts will exult in the LORD.
10.8: I will signal for them and gather them, for I have redeemed them; they will be as numerous as before.
10.9: Though I scattered them among the nations, they will remember me even in distant countries; they will survive with their children and return.
10.10: I will bring them back from Egypt and gather them from Assyria. I will settle them in Gilead and Lebanon until there is no room for them.
10.11: He will pass through the sea of distress and strike down the waves; all the depths of the Nile will dry up. The pride of Assyria will be brought low, and the scepter of Egypt will depart.
10.12: I will strengthen them in the LORD, and they will walk in his name, declares the LORD.

11.1: Open your doors, Lebanon, so that fire may devour your cedars!
11.2: Wail, cypress tree, for the cedar has fallen; the majestic trees are destroyed! Wail, oaks of Bashan, for the dense forest has come down!
11.3: Listen to the cry of the shepherds — their splendor is destroyed! Listen to the roar of the young lions — the jungle of the Jordan is devastated!
11.4: This is what the LORD my God says: Shepherd the flock marked for slaughter.
11.5: Their buyers slaughter them without guilt. Those who sell them say, 'Praise the LORD — I have become rich!' And their own shepherds show them no mercy.
11.6: For I will no longer spare the inhabitants of the land, declares the LORD. I am about to hand each person over to his neighbor and his king. They will devastate the land, and I will not rescue anyone from their hand.
11.7: So I shepherded the flock marked for slaughter — the flock the dealers exploited. I took two staffs, calling one Favor and the other Union, and I shepherded the flock.
11.8: In one month I did away with three shepherds. But I grew impatient with them, and they loathed me in return.
11.9: So I said, 'I will no longer shepherd you. What is dying, let it die; what is to be destroyed, let it be destroyed. Those who remain, let them devour one another.'
11.10: Then I took my staff called Favor and broke it, canceling the covenant I had made with all the peoples.
11.11: It was canceled on that day. The sheep merchants who were watching understood that this was the word of the LORD.
11.12: I said to them, 'If you think it right, pay me my wages; if not, keep them.' So they weighed out thirty pieces of silver as my wages.
11.13: But the LORD said to me, 'Throw it to the potter' — this magnificent sum at which they valued me. So I took the thirty pieces of silver and threw them to the potter in the house of the LORD.
11.14: Then I broke my second staff called Union, canceling the bond of brotherhood between Judah and Israel.
11.15: The LORD said to me, 'Take again the gear of a foolish shepherd.'
11.16: For I am about to raise up a shepherd in the land who will not care for those that are perishing, or seek the straying, or heal the injured, or sustain the healthy — but will devour the flesh of the fat sheep, tearing off even their hooves.
11.17: Woe to the worthless shepherd who abandons the flock! May a sword strike his arm and his right eye! Let his arm be completely withered, his right eye totally blinded!

12.1: An oracle — the word of the LORD concerning Israel. This is the declaration of the LORD, who stretched out the heavens, who laid the foundation of the earth, and who formed the human spirit within a person:
12.2: I am going to make Jerusalem a cup that sends all the surrounding peoples reeling. Judah too will be caught up in the siege against Jerusalem.
12.3: On that day I will make Jerusalem a heavy stone for all the nations. Everyone who tries to lift it will injure themselves severely. All the nations of the earth will assemble against it.
12.4: On that day, declares the LORD, I will strike every horse with panic and every rider with madness. I will watch over the house of Judah, but every horse of the nations I will strike with blindness.
12.5: Then the clans of Judah will say in their hearts, 'The inhabitants of Jerusalem draw their strength from the LORD of Hosts, their God.'
12.6: On that day I will make the clans of Judah like a fire pot among logs, like a flaming torch among sheaves. They will devour all the surrounding peoples on the right and on the left, while Jerusalem remains inhabited in its own place.
12.7: The LORD will save the settlements of Judah first, so that the prestige of the house of David and of Jerusalem's inhabitants does not overshadow Judah.
12.8: On that day the LORD will protect the inhabitants of Jerusalem; even the weakest among them will be like David on that day, and the house of David will be like God — like the angel of the LORD going before them.
12.9: On that day I will set out to destroy all the nations that attack Jerusalem.
12.10: And I will pour out on the house of David and the inhabitants of Jerusalem a spirit of grace and supplication, so that they look on me — on the one they have pierced — and mourn for him, as one mourns for an only son, and weep bitterly over him, as one weeps over a firstborn.
12.11: On that day the mourning in Jerusalem will be as intense as the mourning for Hadad-rimmon in the plain of Megiddo.
12.12: The land will mourn, each family privately: the family of the house of David in private, with the women separately; the family of Nathan in private, with the women separately;
12.13: the family of the house of Levi in private, with the women separately; the family of Shimei in private, with the women separately;
12.14: and all the remaining families, each in private, with the women separately.
